sentence_reviews.text: Neural coding, plasticity, memory, perception, and cognition provide biological inspiration and comparative models of intelligence.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA lists neuroscience and psychology as AI foundations and covers neural networks, perception-adjacent vision/NLP applications, and questions of cognition/consciousness. The exact neural coding, plasticity, memory, and comparative-model claim is not directly supported by the inspected table of contents.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Neuroscience & cognitive science is to connect Probability theory, Dynamical systems & control theory to downstream capabilities including Perceptron, Hebbian, Oja & spike-timing plasticity.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports neuroscience, psychology, control theory, neural networks, gradients/learning, and reinforcement learning. It does not directly mention Hebbian, Oja, or spike-timing plasticity in the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Perceptron, Hebbian, Oja & spike-timing plasticity
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports neural networks and learning as AI topics with neuroscience foundations, but the inspected evidence does not directly support the named plasticity mechanisms.

AIMA supports both neuroscience/psychology and probability/probabilistic reasoning as AI foundations, but it does not directly state that neuroscience and cognition are combined with probability theory.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports neuroscience, psychology, control theory, probability, and discussion of AI limits. It does not directly support the empirical-mismatch warning or dependency clause in this exact form.

sentence_reviews.text: Neuroscience & cognitive science rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports probability as an AI foundation and probabilistic reasoning/learning, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish probability as a dependency of neuroscience and cognitive science broadly.

sentence_reviews.text: Neuroscience & cognitive science rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA lists control theory as an AI foundation and covers planning/control, but it does not directly support the dependency of neuroscience and cognitive science on dynamical systems/control theory.

sentence_reviews.text: Neuroscience & cognitive science rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Signal processing & harmonic analysis.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected packet source does not directly support signal processing or harmonic analysis as a dependency for neuroscience and cognitive science.

edge_reviews.notes: Probability is clearly an AI foundation, but the inspected source does not establish it as a common prerequisite for neuroscience and cognitive science as a whole.
AIMA includes control theory as an AI foundation and covers planning/control, but the specific prerequisite relation to neuroscience/cognition is not directly supported by inspected evidence.
No inspected source evidence supports signal processing and harmonic analysis as a dependency for this node.
